title: When God Is Quiet, He’s Still Working
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this. David cried, How long, Lord? in Psalm 13. Elijah expected wind and fire, but God whispered in a gentle breeze. Jesus waited two days before going to Lazarus, and the delay became a bigger miracle. Silence isn’t absence; it’s strategy. What if the quiet is God training your ears, steadying your heart, and aligning timing you can’t see? Isaiah 30:15 says, In quietness and trust is your strength. Tonight, sit for five unhurried minutes, breathe the name of Jesus, ask one honest question, then listen. Don’t force it. Obey the last clear word. He is nearer than your breath, and the whisper is coming.

title: Why God Seems Silent: The Deeper Work
hook: Heaven feels quiet? It may be growth, not abandonment.
script: Heaven feels quiet? It may be growth, not abandonment. In Scripture, silence often shapes us. Formation: roots go deep when noise dies down. Protection: God hides you like Moses in the cleft when danger rages. Redirection: closed doors steer you like Paul in Acts 16. Invitation: be still; in quietness and trust you find strength (Isaiah 30:15). Try this tonight: replay your day with Jesus. Thank Him for one gift. Notice one heavy moment. Ask, Where were You? Listen. Then choose one small obedience for tomorrow. When you do this, the quiet stops accusing you and starts teaching you. God is working while you wait.

title: A 7-Day Reset for Silent Seasons
hook: If you’re tired of waiting on God, listen to this.
script: If you’re tired of waiting on God, listen to this. Here’s a simple seven-day reset for a silent season. Day one, unplug for ten minutes and just breathe the name of Jesus. Day two, read Psalm 13 slowly and let your ache be prayer. Day three, ask God one clear question and wait. Day four, write the last thing you know He asked and do one small step. Day five, take a silent walk and notice His care. Day six, tell one trusted friend; don’t isolate yourself, Hebrews 10:24-25. Day seven, worship softly and thank Him ahead of time. Pray this: Lord, meet me in the quiet. I’m listening.
